See Policy 11.2.3 above for elements of the built environment that promote historic assets.

goalDetails.detail: Plan, coordinate, and provide for efficient, equitable, and environmentally sound infrastructure to support existing communities and the Comp Plan�s vision for future growth.

policies.policyDescription: Infrastructure Design: Encourage design of visible infrastructure (surface and overhead) that respects the character of neighborhoods and communities and protects significant natural and cultural features. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: See Community Identity Goal 4.3 for character-defining elements of each Community Planning Area.
See Land Use Goal 5.3 for coordination of land use and infrastructure for efficient development patterns.
See Transportation Goal 6.8 for context-sensitive street design.
See Urban Design Goal 7.6 for context-sensitive infrastructure design guidelines.
See Parks & Open Space Goal 10.3 for Open Space policies.
See Heritage Conservation Goal 11.3 for cultural corridors and view protection.

policies.policyDescription: Water and Wastewater Utility: Ensure consistency between Comp Plan and ABCWUA policies by coordinating infrastructure planning and programming. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Follow the east-to-west water zone method of water service delivery on the West Side.
Consider exceptions to the east-to-west water zone method of water service delivery only in areas designated specifically for employment growth.
See Resilience & Sustainability Policy 13.2.2 for water conservation.

policies.policyDescription: Small-scale Water Systems: Protect public health and limit negative environmental impacts. [BC]

subPolicies.text: Regulate private domestic wells, smaller-scale water utilities and private or community on-site wastewater disposal systems to protect public health and prevent contamination of surface and groundwater.
Minimize negative impacts on landforms and natural features and encourage low-impact solutions, including constructed wetlands.

policies.policyDescription: Drainage and Flood Control: Reduce or eliminate flooding by improving ponding and drainage capacities in an environmentally sensitive manner through the development process and in coordination with flood control agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Minimize and mitigate stormwater run-off from development by limiting the amount and extent of impervious surfaces and encouraging landscaped medians and parking swales.
Preserve natural drainage functions of arroyos to the extent possible and use naturalistic design treatment when structural improvements are required for flood control.
Coordinate with the National Park Service and AMAFCA to determine where and how drainage improvements will cross the Petroglyph National Monument.
See Policy 12.4.5 below for Facility Plans.
See Transportation Policy 6.8.1 for roadway location and design that responds to drainage and flood control systems.
See Urban Design Policy 7.6.1 for designing context-sensitive infrastructure.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.4.4 for arroyos and drainage facilities as community green space.
See Resilience & Sustainability Goal 13.2 for policies about water conservation and quality.

policies.policyDescription: Irrigation System: Coordinate with MRGCD and other stakeholders to protect the irrigation system. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: See Policy 12.1.4 above for additional policies on drainage systems.
See Land Use Policies 5.5.3 and 5.5.4 for Rural and Semi-Urban Areas in the County.
See Urban Design Policy 7.6.1 for designing context-sensitive infrastructure.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.4.4 for arroyos and drainage facilities as community green space.
See Heritage Conservation Policy 11.1.3 for protection of acequias as cultural and agricultural heritage.

policies.policyDescription: Energy Systems: Coordinate with energy providers to safeguard essential infrastructure to serve existing development and ensure a safe, adequate, and reliable supply to support growth. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Maintain an economical and environmentally-sound supply of energy through energy conservation and the use of alternative and renewable energy sources.

Communicate with energy service providers about new development proposals and growth trends to optimize the efficiency of the development process and long-term planning for the community�s energy needs.
Promote the cost savings and environmental benefits of energy conservation measures and renewable energy in City and County operations and buildings and in private development.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.8 for solar access protections.
See Resilience & Sustainability Policy 13.4.3 for energy conservation policies.
See also the Electric System Transmission and Generation Facility Plan (2010-2020).

policies.policyDescription: Communication Systems: Support widespread and affordable access to high-quality communication systems in order to maximize flexibility for prospective customers, businesses, and industries. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Coordinate with information technology systems providers to achieve an open-access community broadband network that residents and businesses can connect to throughout the city and county.
See Economic Development Policy 8.2.3 for leveraging public funds to encourage private investment.

goalDetails.detail: Provide community facilities that have convenient access and a wide range of programs for residents from all cultural, age, geographical, and educational groups to enhance quality of life and promote community involvement. [ABC]

policies.policyDescription: Prioritization Process: Assess the resources and needs for community facilities throughout the city and county. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Engage the public to determine priorities and ensure equitable public investment in community facilities.
Foster better communication and cooperation between the City, County, institutions, and residents to make community programs as widely available as possible.
Plan, design, and program community facilities to meet the needs of all age groups.
Engage with community and stakeholder groups to identify local and cultural needs and preferences that can be reflected in facility design and programming.
See Policy 12.4.2 below for ADA compliance in all community facilities.

policies.policyDescription: Existing Facilities: Maintain and improve existing community facilities to better and more equitably serve the community. [ABC]
New Facilities: Site new facilities in areas with excellent access to provide services to underserved and developing areas. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Locate community facilities in designated Centers near retail and/or commercial services to make access to both private and community services more convenient for residents of surrounding neighborhoods or communities.
Ensure site has access for pedestrians, cyclists, and transit users, as well as for drivers.

Pursue opportunities to coordinate, design, and develop new community facilities (including parks) to co-locate or share a site in order to best leverage public investment and maximize accessibility for the public.
Coordinate with public and private schools to locate new schools in Centers or co-located with other compatible public facilities and services.
Design community facilities to realize opportunities for City/County beautification.
See Policy 12.4.2 below for ADA compliance in all community facilities.

goalDetails.detail: Plan, coordinate, and provide efficient, equitable, and environmentally sound services to best serve residents and protect their health, safety, and well-being.

policies.policyDescription: Access to Public Services: Maximize residents� access to public services and distribute services equitably, whether they are provided by the City or County or in partnership with other agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Ensure that the location of public safety facilities provides adequate response rates.
Encourage libraries, schools, and health and social services to locate in Centers to be accessible to pedestrians, cyclists, transit users, and drivers.
Meet the needs of vulnerable and homeless populations with residential care facilities and support services without overburdening individual neighborhoods or communities.
See Goal 12.4 below for policies about co-locating community facilities and public services.

policies.policyDescription: Solid Waste Management: Maintain a clean and healthy community by providing solid waste services. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Minimize potential adverse environmental impacts of collection, transfer, and disposal.
Manage the energy content and material value of municipal solid waste.
Encourage solid waste recycling systems that reduce the volume of waste while converting portions of the waste stream to useful products and/or energy.
Improve management of hazardous waste generated by households.
Design and operate landfills to prevent pollution and improve the land�s potential for open space or reuse.
Monitor former landfills to protect the environment and public�s health and safety.
Collaborate with other agencies to prevent, control, and clean up illegal dumping on public and private property and in waterways.
See Resilience & Sustainability Goal 13.5 for community health policies.

policies.policyDescription: Fire and Rescue: Provide comprehensive fire and rescue and emergency medical services to save and protect lives, property, and the environment in cooperation with the public and other agencies. [ABC]

Police and Sheriff: Maintain a safe and secure community by providing crime prevention, police protection, law enforcement, and investigative services in cooperation with the public and other agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Strengthen relationships with residents and businesses through ongoing community policing and crime prevention efforts.
Prioritize crime reduction efforts in areas with higher crime rates.
See Urban Design Policy 7.3.2 for Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design (CPTED).

policies.policyDescription: Courts and Corrections: Provide an effective and humane criminal justice system supported by adequate budgets and intergovernmental coordination. [BC]
Emergency Management: Improve emergency services delivery and emergency preparedness through coordination between City and County and with other agencies. [ABC]

Libraries: Expand library services as necessary to make them accessible to people on-line or at the neighborhood and community level and create programs in partnership with other departments and institutions to advance educational levels and professional skills. [ABC]
Education: Complement programming provided by educational institutions to expand educational opportunities for residents in all cultural, age, economic, and educational groups. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Partner with other agencies to provide early childhood education.
Provide after-school programs in community facilities and schools, especially for at-risk youth.
Increase adult literacy in collaboration with local schools, nonprofits, and other institutions.
Cooperate with local organizations and institutions to provide workforce training programs.

policies.policyDescription: Behavioral Health Services: Develop a comprehensive behavioral health program that includes substance abuse prevention and a continuum of care for vulnerable populations based on a multi-disciplinary approach and broad inter-agency coordination. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: See Land Use Policy 5.3.7 for distributing objectionable land uses equitably.
See Housing Goals 9.4 and 9.5 for providing housing and services for homeless and vulnerable populations.

goalDetails.detail: Coordinate with other providers to leverage resources, maximize efficiencies, bridge service gaps, and provide added value.

policies.policyDescription: Collaborative Strategies: Develop strategies to meet changing demand over time for public infrastructure, community facilities, and services. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Monitor and track demographics, user preferences, and development trends.
Develop multi-disciplinary approaches to identify and meet community needs.
Coordinate infrastructure planning along corridors and across property owners.
Prioritize infrastructure projects, capital investment, and services in an equitable way to meet the needs of all communities over time.
See Community Identity Goals 4.2 and 4.3 for community engagement through the CPA assessment process.

policies.policyDescription: ADA: Work collaboratively across City and County departments to meet or exceed ADA standards in all public facilities to serve residents of all ages and abilities. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: See Transportation Policy 6.5.2 for ADA considerations specific to transportation infrastructure.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.1.2 for ADA considerations specific to parks and Open Space facilities.

policies.policyDescription: Information Sharing: Coordinate and share information about infrastructure and community facility and service needs across departments, between the City and County, and among public and private agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Promote ongoing department and agency review of development plan submittals to the City and County.
Strengthen communication with area schools and post-secondary institutions to coordinate land use and transportation, plan infrastructure systems, and provide educational and training programs.

policies.policyDescription: Joint Use: Encourage joint use of public facilities, rights-of-way, and easements where uses are compatible and complementary. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Plan and develop co-located community facilities where feasible to avoid duplication of services.
Share facilities and pool resources with other agencies to improve public access to educational, health, and social services in underserved areas and enrich programming.
Where feasible, use easements to provide joint-use corridors to and through communities for power and internet lines, drainage, trails, etc.
Coordinate with agencies, including the State and Federal governments, to co-locate facilities whenever possible.
See Parks & Open Space Goal 10.4 for joint use of parks, Open Space, and recreation facilities.
See Transportation Goal 6.7 for joint use of corridors and transportation infrastructure.

policies.policyDescription: Facility Plans: Develop, update, and implement facility plans for infrastructure systems, such as drainage, electric transmission, natural gas, and information technology that benefit from cross-agency and public-private coordination. [ABC]

goalDetails.detail: Identify and allocate sufficient resources to support infrastructure, community facility, and public service needs in order to invest public dollars efficiently and effectively and to maintain a sound fiscal position.

policies.policyDescription: Annexation: Rationalize jurisdictional boundaries and streamline the provision of services through annexation when the City and County agree. [ABC]
Cost-Benefit Analysis: Evaluate the economic, social, and environmental costs and benefits of potential public infrastructure projects, community facilities, and public services. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Consider impacts on existing users, new service recipients, and the community at large.
Consider fiscal impacts on the City and County.
Consider the life-cycle costs (e.g. design, construction, operation, and maintenance) of infrastructure and community facility projects.
Prioritize public investment for new facilities that co-locate or share a site over stand-alone City and County facilities.

policies.policyDescription: Cost Allocation: Allocate the costs of new community and public facilities and infrastructure extensions and upgrades fairly and equitably to support new development. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Allocate the cost of extending public infrastructure and providing additional public services for new private development to the developer.
Ensure that the impact fee schedule and/or other cost allocation mechanisms are consistent with the Comp Plan vision for future growth and adequately fund new infrastructure and services.
Encourage coordination among property owners to fund infrastructure needed for new development outside of existing public infrastructure service areas.

policies.policyDescription: Funding Strategy: Align public investment and leverage public-private partnerships and bonding capacity in areas consistent with the Comp Plan�s vision for future growth. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Prioritize public investment in existing service areas to expand capacity and rehabilitate public infrastructure systems.
Facilitate investment in Centers, Corridors, and Metropolitan Redevelopment Areas.
Assess the public and fiscal benefit of funding mechanisms, such as Tax Increment Development Districts, Special Assessment Districts, or Public Improvement Districts for proposed development.
See Policy 12.5.1 above for cost-benefit analyses.
See Land Use Goal 5.1 and Policy 5.2.1 for policies about development in Centers and Corridors.
See Land Use Goal 5.3 for policies that promote efficient development patterns.
See Urban Design Policy 7.3.4 for designing for infill development.

policies.policyDescription: Cost Efficiencies: Identify, share, and implement a range of tools to achieve cost-efficiencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Reduce the length of sewer and water lines by encouraging development in areas with existing infrastructure and mixed-use and cluster development.
Invest in and incorporate the most up-to-date technology for infrastructure and utilities.

policies.policyDescription: Staff Capacity: Budget for adequate staff, training, and appropriate technology to plan, deliver, and monitor public services effectively and efficiently. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Ensure opportunities for staff to stay up to date in professional best practices, skills, and technology related to their field. [ABC]
Provide training opportunities in customer service, workplace safety, professional conduct, cultural sensitivity, and racial equity. [A]
See Community Identity Policy 4.2.2 for providing meaningful public engagement processes.

policies.policyDescription: Public Input: Provide information and opportunities for input about capital investment programming, project delivery, and funding priorities. [ABC]

goalDetails.detail: Promote resource-efficient growth and development to help mitigate global climate change and adapt to its local impacts.

policies.policyDescription: Resource-Efficient Development: Promote development in the city and county that works with nature to slow global climate change. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: See Land Use Policy 5.3.4 for conservation development.
See Urban Design Policies 7.4.2 and 7.4.3 on minimizing the environmental impact of off-street parking.
See Urban Design Goal 7.5 and Policy 7.6.1 for site, landscaping, and infrastructure design tailored to climatic conditions.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.4.4 for protecting arroyos and drainage as green space.

policies.policyDescription: Greenhouse Gas Mitigation: Mitigate greenhouse gas emissions in developments and streetscapes. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Expand the tree canopy in developed areas and ensure its long-term health, through landscape regulations for developments and in streetscape improvements.
Accommodate the use of motorized vehicles that run on alternative fuels through zoning and development regulations.
See Policy 13.5.3 below for energy conservation and renewable energy resources.
See Urban Design Goal 7.5 for trees and landscaping.

policies.policyDescription: Public Infrastructure and Facilities: Consider increasing temperatures and other potential impacts of climate change in the design and operation of public infrastructure and community facilities. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Prepare for increasing transportation maintenance and operations expenses and consider future conditions when making decisions about transportation system repairs, replacements, or retrofit.
Locate new community facilities � and move existing assets when feasible � away from vulnerable or sensitive environmental areas.
Prioritize energy and water conservation in the design of public facilities.
Support code requirements for electrification of private commercial and residential buildings
See Policies 13.5.3 and 13.5.4 below about the impacts of infrastructure and facilities on communities.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Goals 12.1 and 12.2 for additional policies on infrastructure and community facilities.

goalDetails.detail: Protect and conserve our region�s limited water supply to benefit the range of uses that will keep our community and ecosystem healthy.

policies.policyDescription: Water Supply: Coordinate with ABCWUA, state, and other agencies to plan and maintain an adequate water supply to meet municipal, agricultural, and ecosystem needs that ensure the overall resilience and sustainability of our community. [ABC]
Water Conservation: Foster the efficient management and use of water in development and infrastructure. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Collaborate across disciplines and agencies to integrate best practices in water management in land use policies and development standards, increase understanding of water-related impacts of development, and ensure regional coordination.
Encourage and support alternative water uses for industrial and commercial sites, including self-sustaining water systems.
Discourage wasteful water use, such as extensive landscape water runoff to uncultivated areas.
Use water harvesting techniques and water reuse systems when possible for trees and landscaping.
Design storm drainage facilities to optimize infiltration and help recharge the aquifer.
Design rights-of-way to slow runoff by using permeable materials where possible and keeping lane widths to the minimum required for safe travel appropriate to the road�s intended capacity.
See Community Identity Goal 4.2 for processes to engage the community in decision making.
See Transportation Goal 6.8 for policies on context-sensitive rights-of-way.
See Urban Design Goal 7.5 on encouraging drought-tolerant plants in development.
See Urban Design Policy 7.6.1 on matching stormwater infrastructure to the surrounding context.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.1.4 for naturalized treatment of arroyos.

policies.policyDescription: Water Quality: Coordinate with the ABCWUA, state, and other agencies to maintain the quality of our groundwater and surface waters. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Follow a total systems approach to water as a valuable resource.
Minimize the potential for contaminants to enter the community�s water supply.
Clean stormwater flows by natural processes before they enter the storm drain system and treatment throughout the stormwater system prior to discharge to MRGCD drains and the river.
Require grading and re-vegetation as appropriate to prevent erosion and sediment deposition during and after construction.
See Urban Design Policy 7.6.1 for stormwater treatment.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.1.4 for drainage and flood control.

goalDetails.detail: Maximize the ability of built and natural environments to withstand natural hazards and recover from adverse events.

policies.policyDescription: Resilient Infrastructure and Structures: Ensure that infrastructure systems and structures are designed, renovated, and maintained to withstand natural hazards. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Build redundancy into critical infrastructure systems.
Coordinate with providers of water, energy, and communication services to minimize service interruptions after adverse events.
Partner with service providers, agencies, and scientific research centers to develop, test, and implement new energy systems and technologies, such as micro-grids.
Ensure structures are built for resistance to regional hazards, including strong winds, floods, and wildfires.
See Urban Design Goal 7.6 for policies on context-sensitive infrastructure.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Goal 12.1 for additional policies on infrastructure.

policies.policyDescription: Flood Mitigation: Prevent flood damage and coordinate flood control and response with other agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Limit development in higher flood risk areas.
Limit the volume of water runoff generated from new development to ensure the viability of down-stream stormwater facilities.
Coordinate stormwater and flood control management with other municipalities in the county, the Albuquerque Metropolitan Arroyo Flood Control District (AMAFCA), and MRGCD.
See Urban Design Policy 7.4.2 for reducing the amount of impervious parking area in developments.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.4.4 regarding arroyos and drains.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.1.4 on flood control.

policies.policyDescription: Wildfire Mitigation: Mitigate the risk of wildfire damage to life and property. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Discourage housing, commercial, and industrial growth adjoining forest and other Open Space by incentivizing development in existing developed areas, including in Centers and along Corridors.
Use roads and other rights-of-way as defensive space to separate homes from Open Space.
Locate minimum transportation infrastructure needed for mobility and evacuation at the interface between wildland and development.
See Heritage Conservation Policy 11.3.1 on single-loaded streets and transitions between Open Space and private development.

policies.policyDescription: Drought Mitigation: Collaborate with the ABCWUA and other water-related agencies to determine best practices for mitigating drought effects and to assist with public education and implementation of water conservation measures. [ABC]
Extreme Heat Mitigation: Mitigate the heat island effect of urban development and coordinate emergency response to extreme heat events with other agencies. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Increase the tree canopy in existing neighborhoods and other developed areas to provide shade for people and mitigate heat radiating from buildings and pavement.
Establish climate-controlled emergency facilities for residents susceptible to heat exhaustion, such as children and the elderly.
See Policy 13.4.3 below for energy conservation measures that also lower heat generated by urban development.
See Urban Design Policies 7.4.1 and 7.4.2 and Goal 7.5 for additional policies on providing shade and reducing impervious area in developments.

goalDetails.detail: Protect, conserve, and enhance natural resources, habitat, and ecosystems.

policies.policyDescription: Air Quality: Maintain good air quality that complies with federal standards to safeguard public health and enhance quality of life for all residents. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Continue to enforce air quality regulations to minimize pollution from particulates including fugitive dust, vehicle emissions, wood-burning in homes, and open burning.
During temperature inversions, which cause smog, reduce air pollution from local sources through methods such as no-burn days.
Protect residents from the risk of toxic air emissions through the permitting process and enforcement.
See Transportation Policy 6.1.3 for reducing auto demand.
See Urban Design Policy 7.5.1 for landscape elements that can help improve air quality.
See Transportation Policy 6.4.2 related to air quality.

policies.policyDescription: Energy Resources: Conserve energy and capitalize on renewable energy resources that are plentiful in our region, especially solar and wind energy. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Encourage renewable energy generation and use in private and public development.
Encourage light-colored and heat-reflecting roofing and building materials.
Maximize energy efficiency for heating, cooling, and lighting systems in public facilities, transit and government vehicles, and street lights.
Incorporate renewable energy technology in city and county facilities, including solar-powered lighting and signage.
Support local and statewide standards for community solar programs and prioritize establishing micro grids and modernizing the electrical grid.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.8 for solar rights protections.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.1.6 related to energy systems.

policies.policyDescription: Unique Landforms and Habitats: Protect areas with unique landforms, and crucial habitat for wildlife, through sensitive urban development or acquisition as Open Space. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Protect crucial habitat on private land, such as next to the Bosque, Far West Mesa and in the East Mountains, by limiting the density and intensity of adjoining development, encouraging wildlife corridors and buffers, and mitigating the impacts of development.
Increase the tree canopy in existing neighborhoods and other developed areas to extend and help connect habitat protected within Open Space.
Promote the use of local native plants in development and along public rights-of-way to provide the best food and shelter for local wildlife.
Where vehicles cross arroyos, provide the shortest possible culvert with a diameter sufficient to allow for the movement of local wildlife.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.4 for conservation development.
See Parks & Open Space Policy 10.3.1 on Open Space acquisition.
See Heritage Conservation Goal 11.3 for policies related to protecting unique landforms including the Rio Grande Bosque, Petroglyph National Monument, Sandia Mountains, and Volcano Mesa.

goalDetails.detail: Protect and maintain safe and healthy environments where people can thrive.

policies.policyDescription: Land Use Impacts: Prevent environmental hazards related to land uses. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Remediate sites that pose a detriment to public health, safety, and welfare to return them to productive use.
Protect public health, safety, and welfare by discouraging incompatible land uses in close proximity, such as housing and industrial activity.

Mitigate potential adverse impacts � including noise, emissions, and glare � of new development on surrounding land uses during and after construction through land use regulations, environmental permitting, and enforcement.

Buffer residential neighborhoods and agricultural land from heavy industry with less intense, non-residential land uses to protect the health and safety of residents, agricultural products, and groundwater, while promoting diverse economic activity.
Encourage environmentally-friendly technologies and processes for industrial activity.
See Policy 13.4.1 above for more general policies on protecting air quality.
See Policies 13.5.3 and 13.5.4 below for potential impacts of existing land uses.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.7 for objectionable land uses.
See Land Use Policy 5.6.4 for transitions between Areas of Change and Consistency.

policies.policyDescription: Healthful Development: Encourage public investments and private development that enhance community health. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Promote family gardens, community gardens, farms, and livestock raising to encourage the availability of local food and to increase food security.

Ensure access to parks and open space for all residents by walking, biking, and driving to provide opportunities for passive and active recreation in the outdoors and encourage healthful connections to nature.
Use landscaping and trees in developments and streetscapes to maintain a healthy environment by providing shade and shelter from winds that carry dust and other particulates.
Prioritize development and maintenance of green spaces, community gardens, and food forests within a 10 minute walk of all residential spaces
See Policies 13.1.2 and 13.4.4 above for greenhouse gas mitigation and protecting unique landforms and habitats.
See Land Use Goal 5.3 for efficient development patterns.
See Transportation Goals 6.2 and 6.3 for policies that ensure safe travel conditions for pedestrians, cyclists, transit users, and drivers.
See Urban Design Policy 7.5.1 for context-sensitive landscaping.
See Parks & Open Space Goal 10.1 for policies on the distribution and universal design of parks and Open Space.

policies.policyDescription: Public Infrastructure Systems and Services: Coordinate with providers to ensure that systems and services do not compromise the health, safety, and welfare of the community. [ABC]

subPolicies.text: Recognize, analyze, and minimize the potential adverse, disproportionate impact on at-risk communities in siting new public infrastructure and services.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.7 for objectionable land uses.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Goal 12.1 for infrastructure provision.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.3.2 for solid waste management.
See Policies 13.2.3 and 13.4.1 above for water and air quality.
See other policies in Goal 13.5 above to encourage healthful development and minimize community impacts of land uses.
See Community Identity Goal 4.2 for processes for community engagement in the planning process.
See Land Use Policy 5.3.7 on distribution of objectionable land uses.
See Land Use Policy 5.7.5 for community engagement processes in the development review process.
See Infrastructure, Community Facilities & Services Policy 12.4.1 for collaborative strategies to meet community needs.
See Appendix E for a description and outline of the Community Planning Area assessment process.
